hints.Only 'Vacate': Both 'Vacate' and 'videte' end in -te, the 2nd person plural imperative ending
hints.'videte' and 'quoniam': 'quoniam' is a conjunction meaning 'because/that' — both 'Vacate' and 'videte' are verbs
hints.Only 'videte': Both 'Vacate' and 'videte' end in -te, the 2nd person plural imperative ending

steps.target: to be still / to be free
choices: to be still / to be free

hints.to empty: 'to empty' is 'vacuare' — 'Vacate' comes from 'vacare' (to be still/free)
hints.to call: 'to call' is 'vocare' — 'Vacate' comes from 'vacare' (to be still/free)

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 2nd pl. would be 'vacatis' — 'Vacate' has the -te imperative ending
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive present 2nd pl. would be 'vacetis' — 'Vacate' is a direct command
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'vacare' — 'Vacate' is the imperative plural form

hints.voco, vocare, vocavi, vocatum: These are the principal parts of 'vocare' (to call)
hints.cado, cadere, cecidi, casum: These are the principal parts of 'cadere' (to fall)
hints.volo, volare, volavi, volatum: These are the principal parts of 'volare' (to fly)

hints.Indicative: Indicative present 2nd pl. would be 'videtis' — 'videte' has the imperative -te ending
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive present 2nd pl. would be 'videatis' — 'videte' is a direct command
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'videre' — 'videte' is the imperative plural form

hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the principal parts of 'vivere' (to live)
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the principal parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the principal parts of 'venire' (to come)

hints.Adverb: Adverbs modify verbs — 'quoniam' introduces a subordinate clause
hints.Preposition: Prepositions govern nouns — 'quoniam' introduces a clause meaning 'that/because'
hints.Pronoun: Pronouns replace nouns — 'quoniam' is a subordinating conjunction

translation: Be still and know that I am God
